---
title: "Version Control & Team Science Fundamentals"
---

As we set out on our synthesis journey, it'll be important to begin with two critically important tools for collaborating: GitHub, and an understanding of the crucial elements of team science. GitHub is an 'actual' tool (i.e., a web-based platform you can visit) but thinking deeply about how and why your team works in the way that it does will also prove to be a helpful component of your process; particularly in navigating interpersonal conflicts or mismatches in communication strategy.

:::{.callout-note icon="false" collapse="false"}
### {{< fa file-pen >}} Pre-Class Preparation

Prior to this lesson, you should do the [workshop preparation steps](https://lter.github.io/workshop-github/#workshop-preparation) for the LTER GitHub workshop for either RStudio or Positron

:::

{{< include topics/external_lter-workshop-github.qmd >}}

{{< include topics/team-sci.qmd >}}

{{< include topics/team-diversity.qmd >}}
